ddls:
CREATE TABLE "universities" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university */,
  "university_name" TEXT /* Name of the university */,
  "uk_ranking" INTEGER /* UK ranking of the university */,
  "tef_rating" TEXT /* TEF rating of the university (e.g., Gold, Bronze) */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the university (number of students) */,
  "established_year" INTEGER /* Year the university was established */,
  "location" TEXT /* City and country where the university is located */,
  "total_faculty" INTEGER /* Total number of faculty members */,
  "total_students" INTEGER /* Total number of students */,
  "research_output" INTEGER /* Annual research output (number of publications, patents, etc.) */,
  "international_students" INTEGER /* Number of international students */,
  "endowment" REAL /* University's endowment in millions of dollars */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website URL */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject (e.g., Medicine, Economics) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the subject */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average starting salary for graduates in the subject */,
  "job_placement_rate" REAL /* Job placement rate for graduates in the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "university_subjects" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the university in the subject */,
  "enrollment" INTEGER /* Number of students enrolled in the subject */,
  "faculty_count" INTEGER /* Number of faculty members teaching the subject */,
  "research_grants" INTEGER /* Total research grants received for the subject */,
  "publications" INTEGER /* Number of publications in the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id", "subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rankings" (
  "ranking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ranking */,
  "ranking_name" TEXT /* Name of the ranking (e.g., UK ranking) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the ranking */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the ranking was published */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ranking_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "university_rankings" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "ranking_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ranking */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the university in the ranking */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the ranking was published */,
  "score" REAL /* Numerical score associated with the ranking */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id", "ranking_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tef_ratings" (
  "tef_rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each TEF rating */,
  "tef_rating_name" TEXT /* Name of the TEF rating (e.g., Gold, Bronze) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the TEF rating */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the TEF rating was awarded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tef_rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "university_tef_ratings" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "tef_rating_id" INTEGER /* ID of the TEF rating */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the TEF rating was awarded */,
  "score" REAL /* Numerical score associated with the TEF rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id", "tef_rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_university_access" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_subject_access" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the university or subject was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was used */,
  "usage_type" TEXT /* Type of usage (e.g., login, search) */,
  "usage_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the system was used */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the usage in seconds */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_usage_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., university ranking, subject ranking) */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the generated report file */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the report file in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
